name: checkpoint-is-ipc
body:
SessionState flows between hooks via SqliteSaver checkpoint (keyed by session_id/thread_id), not by re-reading sessions.db.

Rules:
- `run_session` (UPS) is the full state initializer. `_fresh_state` is a shared fallback also used by `run_gate` (PreToolUse) when no UPS checkpoint exists yet for the session.
- prompt_id, domains, keywords, memories are all in the checkpoint — gate_check and log_tool_usage read them from there, never from DB.
- set_prompt_id writes UUID to checkpoint only (no DB write mid-turn). sessions.db is written once at Stop via run_stop.
- pre_tool_use / run_gate is always fail-open — it never exits 2, even in dev mode.

**Why:** LangServe was stateless (each invoke discarded SessionState). SqliteSaver is cross-process safe and makes the graph the session store.
**How to apply:** When adding new hook logic that needs prior-turn data, read from state (it's in the checkpoint). Never add a DB read in gate_check or log_tool_usage. If no UPS checkpoint exists for a session, run_gate generates a fallback prompt_id (prefixed "fb-").
